informal: If $P$ is a $p$-Sylow subgroup of $G$ and $P \triangleleft G$, prove that $P$ is the only $p$-Sylow subgroup of $G$.
lean:
theorem Herstein_exercise_2_11_6 {G : Type*} [Group G] {p : ℕ} (hp : Nat.Prime p)
  {P : Sylow p G} (hP : P.Normal) :
  ∀ (Q : Sylow p G), P = Q
reasoning: The Lean statement asserts that for every Sylow p-subgroup Q of G, P equals Q, given that P is normal. This is exactly the informal claim that a normal p-Sylow subgroup is the unique p-Sylow subgroup. The `Sylow p G` type encodes p-Sylow subgroups, `P.Normal` matches "P ◁ G", and the prime hypothesis `hp` is standard (and harmless even if redundant since `Sylow p G` is well-defined regardless). The universal quantifier over Q correctly captures uniqueness.
